title: Justification of Manga Entertainments Treatment of EoE
display_title: Justification of Manga Entertainments Treatment of EoE
wikitext:
[]: Justification of Manga Entertainment's Treatment of EoE             Quote Post by Cirk » Mon Nov 17, 2014 8:25 pm Title tweaked to avoid confusion. - SSD Hey, I'm new here, so hello! Not sure if this has ever been talked about before on here so forgive me if this is a duplicate or whatever. Alright, so it seems everyone gets bent out of shape for Manga Entertainment's treatment of End of Evangelion, or more specifically, it being in letterbox format. Now, my theory on why they would do such a thing is the following: EoE was released in 2002 (correct me if I'm wrong), so most people would probably still be viewing it through a 4:3 TV and DVD players may crop 16:9 to fit the screen if their settings were never changed. So, to prevent this: from becoming this: thus making viewers miss the part where Asuka looks at Shinji, Manga Ent did what they did.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Nov 17, 2014 8:38 pm I don’t think this point has ever been argued. That being said, most (if not all) DVD players automatically would adjust any aspect ratio – 4:3, 16:9, or otherwise, – to fit in the TV it’s shown in. Think of the “Fit” option on most computers when it comes to desktop wallpaper. “Fill” would cause the symptom you’re describing above, whereas the “Fit” would shrink a 16:9 to fit a 4:3 screen only when necessary. Of course, this assumes that the DVD player was set to interact with a 4:3 screen. I think many DVD players at the time of this disc release were automatically set for 4:3 TV sets, therefore this wasn't being an issue with many players at the time. This doesn’t negate your point in stating that this still might be a reason for Manga to do this thing, but if what you are arguing truly was the case then it does ultimately shows how little Manga was aware of the technology around them at the time. Though, the Manga Eva releases are generally weird anyway. They didn’t tamper with the image quality of Death as far as I know, yet they bent over backwards to change the way EoE was presented. I’m not sure why they left one untouched while seemingly actively altering the other. (Unless the changes in the image quality of EoE were all accidental, which is another possibility.)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Nov 17, 2014 10:32 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: most (if not all) DVD players automatically would adjust any aspect ratio – 4:3, 16:9, or otherwise, – to fit in the TV it’s shown in

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Mon Nov 17, 2014 10:42 pm You're over-thinking the situation. Manga Entertainment simply used the exact same source master for their 2002 EoE release as the Japanese DVD from 1999 (catalog no. KIBA-105 ), which was similarly 4:3 letterboxed. Of course Manga then filtered it to death, but that's a different story altogether. As to why Gainax's own master was letterboxed, that's because they were using the same source masters on their pre-Renewal DVD releases that they had previously used on their VHS/LD releases ( KIVA-9401 and KILA-9401 , respectively). Obviously 16:9 anamorphic widescreen video didn't exist for the VHS or Laserdisc formats, so the only solution for 1.85:1 material would be to have black bars above and below the picture to pad out the 4:3 frame. That said, even if Gainax hadn't been reusing the VHS/LD master for the DVDs, anamorphic widescreen still wasn't terribly common in the year 1999 even for mainstream Hollywood DVDs. As it turned out, EoE wouldn't get a 16:9 widescreen DVD release until the June 2003 when the Renewal boxset ( KIBA-91001 ) was released in Japan. As such, it would be impossible for Manga's 2002 release to be 16:9 widescreen given the circumstances. And now you know the rest of the story.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Nov 18, 2014 1:18 am Manga made a boo-boo and gave plenty of fans (including myself) a healthy dose of irritation-- but nothing another few watches of EoE won't cure.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Nov 18, 2014 3:51 am Oh, I thought it was a topic about the Sadamoto manga's taking of EoE. *walks away*

[]:             Quote Post by tzigi » Wed Nov 19, 2014 3:27 am ElMariachi wrote: Oh, I thought it was a topic about the Sadamoto manga's taking of EoE. *walks away*
